UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a professional user's profile and company information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

businessName.description: The commercial name of the user's business or professional practice.

taxId.description: The user's tax identification number (CIF/NIF), optional.

address.description: The physical address of the business.

city.description: The city where the business is located.

country.description: The country where the business is located.

phone.description: The contact phone number for the business.

logoUrl.description: URL to the company's logo, optional.

defaultTerms.description: Default general terms and conditions for quotes, stored as rich text or markdown.

nextQuoteNumber.description: The next number to be used for a quote, for generating quote numbers like '2024-0001'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client to whom a quote is issued.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this client. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Client)

companyName.description: The name of the client's company or individual's full name.

contactName.description: The name of the primary contact person at the client's company.

address.description: The client's billing or service address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

Quote.title: Quote
Quote.description: Represents a professional quote or estimate generated by a user for a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quote entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this quote. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Quote)

clientId.description: Reference to the Client for whom this quote is generated. (Relationship: Client 1:N Quote)

title.description: A descriptive title for the quote (e.g., 'Presupuesto Reforma Baño').

quoteNumber.description: Automatically generated and configurable quote number (e.g., 2026-0001).

issueDate.description: The date when the quote was issued.

validUntilDate.description: The date until which the quote is valid.

status.description: The current status of the quote (e.g., Draft, Sent, Accepted, Rejected).

subtotal.description: The sum of all line item prices before discounts and taxes.

totalDiscount.description: The total amount of all applied discounts across the quote.

totalTax.description: The total amount of all applied taxes across the quote.

finalTotal.description: The final total amount of the quote after all calculations.

currency.description: The currency used for the quote (e.g., 'EUR', 'USD').

defaultTermsOverride.description: Specific terms and conditions for this quote, if different from the user's default.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quote was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the quote was last updated.

QuoteSection.title: QuoteSection
QuoteSection.description: Organizes line items within a quote into logical groups (e.g., Materials, Labor).

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuoteSection entity.

quoteId.description: Reference to the Quote this section belongs to. (Relationship: Quote 1:N QuoteSection)

name.description: The name of the section (e.g., 'Materiales', 'Mano de Obra').

description.description: An optional description for the section, possibly AI-generated.

order.description: The display order of the section within its quote.

subtotal.description: The sum of all line item prices within this section before discounts and taxes.

totalDiscount.description: The total amount of all applied discounts within this section.

totalTax.description: The total amount of all applied taxes within this section.

sectionTotal.description: The final total amount for this section after all calculations.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quote section was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the quote section was last updated.

QuoteItem.title: QuoteItem
QuoteItem.description: Represents an individual line item or service within a quote section.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuoteItem entity.

quoteSectionId.description: Reference to the QuoteSection this item belongs to. (Relationship: QuoteSection 1:N QuoteItem)

concept.description: A brief concept or title for the line item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item or service, possibly AI-generated.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the item (e.g., 'h' for hours, 'ud' for units, 'm2').

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item.

discountValue.description: The value of the discount (e.g., '10' for 10% or 10 EUR fixed).

taxRate.description: The applicable tax rate (e.g., 0.21 for 21% IVA).

lineTotal.description: The calculated total for this line item, including quantity, unit price, discount, and tax.

order.description: The display order of the item within its section.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quote item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the quote item was last updated.

QuoteEvent.title: QuoteEvent
QuoteEvent.description: Logs significant actions or status changes related to a quote, forming an activity timeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuoteEvent entity.

quoteId.description: Reference to the Quote this event is associated with. (Relationship: Quote 1:N QuoteEvent)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed the action, if applicable. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N QuoteEvent)

eventDescription.description: A detailed description of the event (e.g., 'Status changed from Draft to Sent').

timestamp.description: The exact time when the event occurred.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quote event was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the quote event was last updated.

Template.title: Template
Template.description: Defines a reusable structure for creating quotes quickly, either pre-defined or user-created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Template entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this template. Null for system-wide templates. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Template)

name.description: The name of the template (e.g., 'Obra/Reformas', 'My Custom Web Design Template').

description.description: A brief description of what this template is used for.

isSystemTemplate.description: Indicates if this is a system-provided template (true) or a user-created template (false).

defaultTermsOverride.description: Specific terms and conditions for quotes created from this template, if any.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the template was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the template was last updated.

TemplateSection.title: TemplateSection
TemplateSection.description: Defines a section within a template, mirroring QuoteSection structure for reusability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TemplateSection entity.

templateId.description: Reference to the Template this section belongs to. (Relationship: Template 1:N TemplateSection)

name.description: The name of the section (e.g., 'Materiales', 'Fases del Proyecto').

description.description: An optional description for the template section.

order.description: The default display order of the section within its template.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the template section was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the template section was last updated.

TemplateItem.title: TemplateItem
TemplateItem.description: Defines an individual line item or service within a template section for quick quote generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TemplateItem entity.

templateSectionId.description: Reference to the TemplateSection this item belongs to. (Relationship: TemplateSection 1:N TemplateItem)

concept.description: A brief concept or title for the template line item.

description.description: A detailed description of the template item or service.

quantity.description: The default quantity for the item.

unit.description: The default unit of measurement for the item (e.g., 'h', 'ud', 'm2').

unitPrice.description: The default price per unit of the item.

discountType.description: The default type of discount applied (e.g., 'percentage', 'fixed').

discountValue.description: The default value of the discount.

order.description: The default display order of the item within its template section.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the template item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the template item was last updated.

ReusableBlock.title: ReusableBlock
ReusableBlock.description: Represents a user-defined reusable line item or service 'block' that can be quickly added to quotes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReusableBlock entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this reusable block. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ReusableBlock)

name.description: A user-friendly name for the reusable block (e.g., 'Standard Web Hosting Package').

concept.description: The default concept for the line item within this block.

description.description: The default detailed description for the line item.

quantity.description: The default quantity for the item.

unit.description: The default unit of measurement for the item.

unitPrice.description: The default price per unit of the item.

discountType.description: The default type of discount applied (e.g., 'percentage', 'fixed').

discountValue.description: The default value of the discount.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the reusable block was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the reusable block was last updated.

AppConfig.title: AppConfig
AppConfig.description: Stores global application configuration settings, like feature limits.

anonymousQuoteLimit.description: The maximum number of quotes an anonymous (guest) user can create.

registeredQuoteLimit.description: The maximum number of quotes a registered user can create.

anonymousBlockLimit.description: The maximum number of reusable blocks an anonymous (guest) user can create.

registeredBlockLimit.description: The maximum number of reusable blocks a registered user can create.

definition.description: Stores the professional profile and company information for each user. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'id' field within the document must match the {userId} path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores clients associated with a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'userId' field in the document must match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Stores professional quotes generated by a user. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'userId' field in the document must match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence. This collection also supports sharing via a 'shareableLinkToken'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the quote.

params.name: quoteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quote.

definition.description: Organizes line items within a specific quote. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. This document explicitly includes the denormalized 'userId' from its parent Quote for Authorization Independence, besides 'quoteId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent quote.

params.name: quoteId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent quote.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quote section.

definition.description: Represents individual line items within a quote section. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. This document explicitly includes the denormalized 'userId' and 'quoteId' from its parent QuoteSection and Quote for Authorization Independence, besides 'quoteSectionId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the grandparent quote.

params.name: quoteId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent quote.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent quote section.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quote item.

definition.description: Logs significant actions and status changes for a specific quote. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'userId' and 'quoteId' fields in the document match the path parameters, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent quote.

params.name: quoteId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent quote.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quote event.

definition.description: Stores user-defined reusable templates for creating quotes. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'userId' field in the document must match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the template.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's template.

definition.description: Defines sections within a user-defined template. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. This document explicitly includes the denormalized 'userId' from its parent Template for Authorization Independence, besides 'templateId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the parent template.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent template.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template section.

definition.description: Defines individual line items within a user-defined template section. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. This document explicitly includes the denormalized 'userId' and 'templateId' from its parent TemplateSection and Template for Authorization Independence, besides 'templateSectionId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the grandparent template.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent template.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent template section.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template item.

definition.description: Stores user-defined reusable line item 'blocks' for quick quote generation. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'userId' field in the document must match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns the reusable block.

params.name: blockId
params.description: The unique identifier for the reusable block.

definition.description: Stores system-provided, globally readable templates for creating quotes. These templates are read-only for all authenticated users and do not contain a 'userId' field as they are not user-owned. This collection facilitates QAPs by segregating public/system data from private user data.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the system template.

definition.description: Defines sections within system-provided templates. These are read-only for all authenticated users and do not contain a 'userId' field. The 'templateId' field in the document matches the path parameter.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent system template.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template section.

definition.description: Defines individual line items within system-provided template sections. These are read-only for all authenticated users and do not contain a 'userId' field. The 'templateId' and 'templateSectionId' fields in the document match the path parameters.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent system template.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent template section.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template item.

definition.description: Stores global application configuration. The 'limits' document contains quote creation limits for different user roles. It should be readable by all authenticated users but only writable by admins.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the mandated core design principles and strategies. It primarily leverages a hierarchical path structure combined with strategic denormalization to achieve Authorization Independence and enable secure QAPs.

1.  Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:
    *   Every user-owned entity (UserProfile, Client, Quote, ReusableBlock, user-specific Templates) is directly nested under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`. This automatically ties the data to `request.auth.uid` via the path parameter, making basic ownership checks straightforward (`allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`).
    *   For subcollections whose access is dependent on the `UserProfile` owner (e.g., QuoteSection, QuoteItem, user-specific TemplateSection, TemplateItem), the `userId` is explicitly denormalized into each document. This means a `QuoteSection` document, for instance, will contain its `quoteId` and also the `userId` of the `UserProfile` who owns the parent `Quote`. This eliminates the need for expensive and rule-breaking `get()` calls to parent documents within security rules, allowing atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. For example, a rule for `QuoteSection` can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to fetch the parent `Quote` or `UserProfile`.

2.  QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:
    *   Data is segregated based on its security posture:
        *   **Private User Data:** All user-specific data (UserProfile, Clients, Quotes, ReusableBlocks, user-defined Templates and their subcollections) are stored under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`. This ensures that `list` operations (queries) for these collections inherently filter by the authenticated user's ID. A user can only query their own data (`/userProfiles/my-uid/clients`) and cannot accidentally or maliciously query another user's data (`/userProfiles/another-uid/clients`). This makes `list` operations secure by construction, as the path itself acts as the primary filter.
        *   **System-wide Data:** System-provided templates and their subcollections (`/systemTemplates/{templateId}/...`) are stored in separate top-level collections. These are intended to be globally readable by any authenticated user but are immutable by users. This segregation clearly defines their access patterns (read-only for all authenticated users) and simplifies their respective rules, making `list` operations on these collections safe and efficient.
    *   The consistent use of path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/entity/{entityId}`) ensures that every document's ownership is explicitly defined by its location, in addition to the global `/appConfig` collection that is readable by authenticated users and only writeable by admins.

3.  Clarity of Intent & Predictability:
    *   Path wildcards are semantic (e.g., `{userId}`, `{quoteId}`, `{sectionId}`).
    *   A dedicated `status` field is used in `Quote` for clear state modeling.
    *   All authorization-relevant fields like `userId` are consistently named.

This architecture ensures that security rules will be simple, performing checks primarily against `request.auth.uid` and `resource.data.userId` (or simply `request.auth.uid == userId` for path-based authorization), without complex or performance-impacting `get()` operations, thereby meeting all core design principles.
